name: Turkish Armed Forces (TAF / TSK)
detail: 2nd largest standing army in NATO with ~355,000 active personnel. Consists of Land Forces, Naval Forces, Air Forces, and Gendarmerie. Historically self-appointed guardian of Kemalist secular principles; underwent dramatic Gülenist-driven purges post-2016 coup attempt, reshaping command structure entirely.

name: F-16 Fighting Falcons
detail: Turkey operates approximately 240 F-16C/D Block 30/40/50 aircraft — the backbone of Turkish Air Force. Turkey was removed from the F-35 program in 2019 following the S-400 purchase. US approved F-16 upgrade package ($20 billion) in 2024 after Turkey lifted veto on Sweden's NATO membership, including 40 new F-16 Block 70 aircraft.

name: S-400 Triumf Air Defense System
detail: Four S-400 batteries delivered from Russia beginning July 2019. Purchase caused Turkey's expulsion from F-35 program and CAATSA sanctions in December 2020. Systems remain in storage/limited operation; not integrated into NATO air defense architecture. Purchase represents Turkey's most consequential strategic decision in decades.

name: TB2 Bayraktar Tactical UAV
detail: Domestically developed armed drone manufactured by Baykar (Erdoğan son-in-law Selçuk Bayraktar). Proved decisive in Syria (2020), Libya (2019-20), and especially Ukraine (2022) conflict. Exported to 30+ countries. Represents Turkey's successful push for defense industry autonomy. Cost ~$1-2 million vs. similar US/Israeli systems at $10-20 million.

name: Land Forces Armor: Leopard 2A4 / M60T
detail: Turkey's land forces include ~354 Leopard 2A4 (Germany) and ~170 M60T Sabra (upgraded by Israel) main battle tanks, plus ~1,300 M60A3 and ~1,000 M48 older models. Several Leopard 2A4 tanks were destroyed in Syria's Azaz area in 2016–17, exposing vulnerabilities against ATGMs. Domestic Altay MBT in development.

name: Turkish Navy — Frigates & Submarines
detail: Turkish Naval Forces operate 16 frigates (MilGem class, GENESIS class, Oliver Hazard Perry class), 12 submarines (209/214 class), and numerous corvettes and patrol boats. Controls Bosphorus and Dardanelles — the world's most strategic straits under 1936 Montreux Convention. Turkey blocked Russian warship passage to Black Sea under Montreux after 2022 Ukraine invasion.

name: Special Forces Command (Bordo Bereliler)
detail: Elite Turkish Special Forces units deployed extensively in PKK counterinsurgency operations, Syria (Operation Euphrates Shield, Olive Branch), and Iraq (Operation Claw series). 'Bordo Bereliler' (Maroon Berets) are Turkey's primary direct-action and counterterrorism units. Received significant capability upgrades post-2001 with US cooperation.

name: MİT (National Intelligence Organization)
detail: Turkey's primary intelligence agency. Played key role in capturing Öcalan (1999), carrying out cross-border operations against PKK in Iraq and Syria, and — controversially — was accused of supporting jihadist groups in Syria 2012-2015. MİT chief Hakan Fidan served until 2023 when he became Foreign Minister. Directly under presidential authority since 2017.

name: T129 ATAK Attack Helicopter
detail: Domestically developed attack helicopter produced by Turkish Aerospace Industries (TAI). 77 aircraft ordered for Turkish Land Forces. Used in PKK counterinsurgency operations in eastern Turkey and northern Iraq. US blocked export of LHTEC T800 engines to third parties due to Turkey's S-400 purchase, delaying international sales (e.g., to Pakistan).

name: İncirlik Air Base (Adana)
detail: Major NATO air base hosting US Air Force units and ~50 B61 nuclear gravity bombs under NATO nuclear sharing arrangement. Used for US operations against ISIS from 2015. Turkey temporarily suspended US access during and after July 2016 coup attempt as political leverage. The base's future has been a recurring point of US-Turkey tension.
